_meta.israeli_category_system: Israel uses T/C notation: T=THC%, C=CBD%. Categories: T20+/C1-5 = THC-dominant; T1/C20+ = CBD-dominant; T10-12/C10-12 = Balanced. Sub-categories validated per IMCA 2022.

receptors.CB1: full agonist (primary target — CNS, brain, spinal cord)

dose_response.note: Biphasic: low doses anxiolytic, high doses anxiogenic; individual sensitivity varies widely (CYP2C9 metabolism)
dose_response.low_mg_5_10: Mild analgesia, slight mood shift, minimal psychoactivity in tolerant adults
dose_response.medium_mg_10_25: Therapeutic window for most indications; some psychoactivity
dose_response.high_mg_25_plus: Risk of anxiety, paranoia, cognitive impairment; therapeutic for severe pain/cancer with tolerance

cannabinoids.metabolism: CYP2C9, CYP3A4 -> 11-OH-THC (active, potent) -> THC-COOH (inactive metabolite, detected in drug testing for weeks)
drug_interactions: antidepressants (complex; SSRI + THC generally safe; MAOIs avoid)
contraindications: personal or family history of schizophrenia/psychosis
children (except seizure disorders under specialist supervision)
history of cannabis use disorder without psychiatric support
cannabinoids.pregnancy_note: Strong evidence of fetal neurodevelopmental harm; NEVER use in pregnancy

negative: sedation at high doses (not all users)
diarrhea/GI upset at very high doses
potential liver enzyme elevation (drug-drug interaction risk at pharmaceutical doses)

dose_response.note: Inverted-U dose response for some effects (anxiety: optimal ~150-300mg single dose; seizures: 10-20mg/kg/day)
dose_response.low_mg_5_25: Mild anxiolytic; may be alerting in some; anti-inflammatory support
dose_response.medium_mg_25_100: Anxiolytic, anti-inflammatory, sleep improvement; therapeutic for anxiety/sleep
dose_response.high_mg_100_300: Anti-anxiety (social phobia RCT dose); sleep induction; anti-psychotic
dose_response.pharmaceutical_mg_300_1500: Epidiolex range for epilepsy; drug interactions at this dose require medical supervision

drug_interactions: clobazam (increases active metabolite 3x — reduce benzo dose)
valproate (additive liver toxicity risk at high CBD doses)
contraindications: active liver disease at pharmaceutical doses
concurrent strong CYP3A4/2C19 inhibitors without monitoring
cannabinoids.israel_approval_2023: CBD-dominant products (T0-T3 / C15-C30) approved for: epilepsy (primary), anxiety (secondary indication), sleep, autism (under research protocol)

cannabinoids.origin: THC degradation product (heat, light, air exposure)

receptors.CB1: weak partial agonist (5-10x weaker than THC)

clinical_relevance.sleep: Often present in aged/exposed cannabis; may contribute to sedative effect of older batches; Steep Hill study claimed CBN most sedative cannabinoid (disputed by Russo) — mechanism unclear but empirically observed
clinical_relevance.pain: Analgesic in animal models; synergy with THC in rat study (Finn et al. 2004)

cannabinoids.formation_note: Increases with storage, heat, UV exposure. Old flower = more CBN. Vaporizer high temps (185°C+) may increase CBN contribution.
cannabinoids.israeli_product_note: Not typically labeled separately in Israeli T/C system but present in all aged or heat-treated flower

cannabinoids.origin: Precursor molecule to THC, CBD, CBC; present in highest concentration in young (immature) cannabis plants

clinical_relevance.ibd: Reduces inflammation in IBD models (Borrelli et al. 2013, PLoS ONE — murine colitis significant reduction)
clinical_relevance.neurodegeneration: Neuroprotective in Huntington's disease models; anti-oxidant
clinical_relevance.antibacterial: MRSA active (Appendino et al. 2008 — same study as CBN)
clinical_relevance.bladder: Inhibits bladder contractions; potential for OAB/MS bladder

cannabinoids.israeli_note: Emerging CBG-rich products appearing in Israel 2023-2024; IMCA evaluating therapeutic protocols

receptors.TRP_channels: TRPA1, TRPV1, TRPV3, TRPV4 agonist — pain transduction

clinical_relevance.depression: Promotes neurogenesis; anti-depressant in animal models
clinical_relevance.entourage: Part of full-spectrum superiority over isolate; synergizes with THC for analgesia (McPartland & Russo 2001)

cannabinoids.note: Raw form of THC in fresh plant; converts to THC via decarboxylation (heat/time). NOT psychoactive until heated.
cannabinoids.clinical_relevance: Raw cannabis juice contains THCA; anti-inflammatory without psychoactivity; relevant for autoimmune applications
cannabinoids.conversion: THCA → THC via decarboxylation at 105°C; ~30 min in oven or immediate via vaporizer/smoke

clinical_relevance.nausea: More potent anti-nausea than CBD in animal models; relevant for CINV; heat-sensitive so fresh plant only
clinical_relevance.anxiety: 5-HT1A affinity higher than CBD (Bolognini et al. 2013)
clinical_relevance.note: Destroyed by heating → must use raw/cold-extracted cannabis products

product_categories.note: Israeli MOH T/C classification as used on licensed pharmacy dispensaries

categories.primary_use: Chronic pain, PTSD (nighttime), sleep, appetite, advanced oncology, MS spasticity
categories.patient_profile: Experienced users; high tolerance; severe indications
categories.caution: High psychoactivity; driving prohibited; tolerance risk

categories.primary_use: Pain (moderate), PTSD (breakthrough), sleep onset, mild-moderate spasticity
Neuropathic pain, MS spasticity (Sativex-equivalent), cancer pain, anxiety with comorbid pain, daytime pain management
categories.patient_profile: Most appropriate starting point for naïve patients with pain+anxiety
categories.note: Most similar to GW Pharma Sativex (EU/Canadian approved); best evidence base for cancer pain (Johnson 2010)

categories.primary_use: Anxiety, epilepsy adjunct, inflammation, IBD maintenance, autism, PTSD daytime, Parkinson's
categories.timing: Morning and afternoon; low impairment risk
categories.patient_profile: CBD seekers with mild THC entourage need; elderly, pediatric; anxiety-primary
categories.note: Small THC amount (T3) provides entourage effect without significant psychoactivity in most adults

categories.primary_use: Pediatric epilepsy, CBD-sensitive anxiety, anti-inflammatory, non-psychoactive maintenance, driving-permitted patients
categories.patient_profile: Children, driving patients, very THC-sensitive, anxiety-only, epilepsy primary
categories.note: Epidiolex equivalent; highest CBD concentration products on Israeli market

categories.primary_use: Pain with moderate psychoactivity; appetite; mood; transition category
categories.patient_profile: Intermediate pain patients; not pure CBD seekers; not severe indication

entourage_effect.concept: The synergistic interaction of multiple cannabinoids, terpenes, and flavonoids producing superior effects compared to isolated compounds. Most robustly evidenced by: (1) CBD reducing THC side effects; (2) terpenes modifying cannabinoid receptor pharmacology; (3) full-spectrum oils outperforming isolates in multiple studies
key_evidence: McPartland & Russo 2001: Multiple terpenes interact with cannabinoid receptors
Gallily et al. 2015: Full-spectrum CBD extract far outperformed CBD isolate at matched doses for inflammation
Ben-Shabat et al. 1998: First description of entourage effect; CBD + CBG + CBN inactive alone but active in combination
Russo 2011: Terpene profiles predict clinical effect differences between strains beyond cannabinoid content alone
entourage_effect.clinical_implication: Israeli licensed pharmacy strains (full flower or full-spectrum oil) should be preferred over CBD isolate for most indications. Pharmaceutical isolates (Epidiolex) are appropriate for pediatric epilepsy where precise dosing outweighs entourage benefit.
entourage_effect.he: אפקט 'הלהקה' — השלם גדול מסכום חלקיו. שמן מלא-ספקטרום > CBD בודד בעד 6x (Gallily 2015). הסיבה שזנים שונים מרגישים שונה אפילו באותם אחוזי THC.
